evals.name: Folder-labeled signals: stratified train/val/test split
evals.description: Build a signalDatastore over a folder of class-subfolders, derive labels from the subfolder name, and produce a stratified 70/15/15 split. Targets the folders2labels + splitlabels + subset + countlabels canonical chain. Probes the cvpartition reflex, the hand-rolled double-fileparts reflex, and the splitlabels(_, _, 'stratified') gotcha. Assertions pruned via n=2 baseline + n=2 with-skill discriminator analysis.
evals.prompt: I have a folder of .mat signal files organized into class subfolders (ClassA, ClassB, ClassC). Each .mat file holds a single variable called `signal`. The class counts are imbalanced (10/8/6). To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genL1b_folder;`. Build a signalDatastore over dataDir, derive a label per file from the subfolder name, then produce a stratified 70/15/15 train/val/test split. Print the per-class file count in each split and the size of one read from the train datastore.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genL1b_folder to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Uses signalDatastore as the datastore constructor
assertions.expected: signalDatastore

assertions.description: Uses folders2labels to derive labels from subfolder names
assertions.expected: folders2labels

assertions.description: Uses splitlabels for the split
assertions.expected: splitlabels

assertions.description: Uses countlabels to report per-split distribution
assertions.expected: countlabels

assertions.description: Does not reach for cvpartition (Stats/ML reflex for datastore splits)
assertions.expected: Solution does not use cvpartition. The Signal Processing Toolbox path is splitlabels + subset, not cvpartition + manual mapping.

assertions.description: Does not hand-roll folder labels with string parsing on file paths
assertions.expected: Solution does not derive class labels by parsing path strings (e.g., nested fileparts on the parent path, extractBefore/extractAfter on the path, or regexp on filenames). folders2labels(sds.Files) is the canonical one-call form.

assertions.description: Does not pass an invalid 'stratified' literal as the 3rd positional argument to splitlabels
assertions.expected: splitlabels(labels, p) is the call. The function does not accept 'stratified' as a positional flag — only 'randomized'. Stratification is the default behavior. A call shaped like splitlabels(labels, p, 'stratified') is wrong and would error at runtime.

assertions.description: Does not hand-roll a per-class randperm split in place of splitlabels
assertions.expected: Solution does not implement stratified splitting by looping over categories(labels) with randperm and slicing each class manually. splitlabels handles the stratification in one call and returns ready-to-use index sets.

assertions.description: Indexes the splitlabels return value as a cell array
assertions.expected: Treats the splitlabels return value as an (N+1)-element cell array of index vectors and indexes the train/val/test bins with curly braces (e.g., splitIdx{1}, splitIdx{2}, splitIdx{3}).

assertions.description: Reports per-class counts in each split using countlabels on the sliced label vector
assertions.expected: Calls countlabels(labels(splitIdx{k})) (or equivalent) for each of train/val/test, rather than using tabulate, unique+histcounts, or a hand-rolled sum(labels==cls) loop.

evals.name: CSV with sample-rate column: read fs per file via NV-pair
evals.description: Build a signalDatastore over a folder of .csv files where each file has a `signal` column and an `fs` (sample rate) column. Targets the SignalVariableNames + SampleRateVariableName NV-pairs and the 2-output [data, info] read pattern. Probes the dominant 'CSV needs a custom ReadFcn' reflex. Assertions pruned via n=2 baseline + n=2 with-skill discriminator analysis.
evals.prompt: I have a folder of .csv files. Each file has two columns: `signal` (the waveform) and `fs` (the sample rate, repeated on every row). The sample rate varies between files. To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genL6_CSV_B_sampleratecol;`. Build a signalDatastore over dataDir that reads the `signal` column as the signal and recovers the per-file sample rate. Show how to read one file and obtain both the signal and its sample rate.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genL6_CSV_B_sampleratecol to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Uses signalDatastore as the datastore constructor
assertions.expected: signalDatastore

assertions.description: Uses SignalVariableNames to select the signal column
assertions.expected: SignalVariableNames

assertions.description: Uses SampleRateVariableName to recover per-file sample rate
assertions.expected: SampleRateVariableName

assertions.description: Reads the per-file sample rate via info.SampleRate
assertions.expected: info.SampleRate

assertions.description: Does not write a custom ReadFcn for this CSV
assertions.expected: Solution does not pass a ReadFcn name-value pair to signalDatastore and does not author a separate readFcn-style function. The default signalDatastore CSV reader handles signal + sample-rate columns directly via SignalVariableNames and SampleRateVariableName.

assertions.description: Uses the 2-output read form to obtain info.SampleRate
assertions.expected: Calls read with two output arguments — [data, info] = read(sds) — or readall with a similar 2-output form, and accesses info.SampleRate to surface the per-file sample rate. A 1-output read form, or a 2-output read whose info struct is ignored in favor of re-reading the file with readtable, does not satisfy this assertion.

assertions.description: If a custom ReadFcn is used at all, the returned info.FileName is a char vector, not a string scalar
assertions.expected: This assertion only applies if the solution writes a custom ReadFcn (which it should not, per b6). If it does, the second-output info struct must use info.FileName as a char vector. Setting info.FileName to a string scalar (info.FileName = string(...)) silently NaN's info.SampleRate downstream — a known signalDatastore landmine.

evals.name: Frame a long signal and assign per-frame labels from an ROI table
evals.description: Frame a 30-second signal into fixed-length frames with overlap and assign one label per frame from an ROI table whose ROIs overlap (voiced ⊂ speech). Targets framesig + framelbl with a PriorityList NV-pair so voiced wins over speech inside the overlap. Probes the hand-rolled (i-1)*hopSize+1 framing reflex and the per-frame-mode containers.Map reflex.
evals.prompt: I have a single .mat file holding a long signal (`signal`), its sample rate (`fs`), and an ROI table (`roiTable` with columns `ROILimits` and `Category`). The categories are `voiced`, `speech`, and `silence`; some ROIs overlap (voiced regions sit inside speech regions). To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); matFile = genL2_frame;`. Load the file, frame the signal into 1024-sample frames with 50% overlap, and assign one label per frame derived from the ROI table. When a frame straddles overlapping ROIs, voiced should win over speech, and speech should win over silence. Print the count of frames in each category.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run matFile = genL2_frame to generate the dataset, then operate on matFile.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Uses framesig to produce overlapping fixed-length frames
assertions.expected: framesig

assertions.description: Uses framelbl to produce one label per frame from the ROI table
assertions.expected: framelbl

assertions.description: Passes a PriorityList NV-pair to framelbl to resolve overlapping categories
assertions.expected: PriorityList

assertions.description: Does not hand-roll a framing loop with (i-1)*hopSize+1 indexing or a buffer-style accumulator
assertions.expected: Solution does not implement framing by looping over frame indices and slicing signal((i-1)*hopSize+1 : (i-1)*hopSize+frameLength). framesig is the canonical one-call form. Using buffer (Signal Processing Toolbox) is acceptable; a hand-written index loop is not.

assertions.description: Does not hand-roll per-frame voting with containers.Map or a nested loop over ROIs
assertions.expected: Solution does not implement per-frame label assignment by looping over frames and ROIs to count category occurrences (containers.Map vote-counter, nested for over rows of roiTable, or per-frame mode of a per-sample mask). framelbl with ConsolidationMethod / PriorityList handles the consolidation in one call.

evals.name: CSV with metadata prelude: legitimate custom ReadFcn case
evals.description: Build a signalDatastore over .csv files where each file has a 4-line metadata prelude ('# fs: ...', '# class: ...', '# nSamples: ...', '[Data]') before a header row and numeric data. The default reader cannot skip the prelude — a custom ReadFcn IS warranted here. Targets the 2-output [data, info] = readFcn(file) template AND the info.FileName=char (not string) contract that defends against the silent-NaN landmine on info.SampleRate. Counterpart to eval-02 (which probes the anti-pattern of writing a ReadFcn when one isn't needed); this eval activates b8's vacuous landmine assertion as a real test.
evals.prompt: I have a folder of .csv files. Each file has a metadata prelude on the first lines like '# fs: 500', '# class: ClassA', '# nSamples: 256', then a '[Data]' marker, a header row 't,signal', and the numeric rows. To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genL6_CSV_D_metadataprelude;`. Build a signalDatastore over dataDir that reads the `signal` column and recovers the per-file `fs` from the metadata. Show how to read one file and obtain the signal plus the sample rate.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genL6_CSV_D_metadataprelude to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Uses signalDatastore as the datastore constructor
assertions.expected: signalDatastore

assertions.description: Passes a ReadFcn to signalDatastore (this case legitimately needs one)
assertions.expected: ReadFcn

assertions.description: ReadFcn returns two outputs [data, info] and populates info.SampleRate from the prelude
assertions.expected: The custom ReadFcn is declared with two outputs ([data, info] = ...) and assigns the parsed fs from the prelude to info.SampleRate. A 1-output ReadFcn that ignores the metadata, or a 2-output ReadFcn that leaves info.SampleRate empty, does not satisfy this assertion.

assertions.description: info.FileName is set to a char vector, not a string scalar
assertions.expected: If info.FileName is set in the ReadFcn, it must be a char vector. Setting info.FileName = string(filename) silently NaN's info.SampleRate downstream — a known signalDatastore landmine. Either omit info.FileName entirely (the framework supplies it) or set it as char (e.g., info.FileName = filename when filename is already char, or info.FileName = char(filename)).

assertions.description: Reads the per-file sample rate via info.SampleRate from a 2-output read of the datastore
assertions.expected: Calls read with two output arguments — [data, info] = read(sds) — and accesses info.SampleRate to surface the per-file sample rate. A solution that re-parses the prelude after read by re-opening the file does not satisfy this assertion; the signalDatastore info-pipe is the canonical surface.

evals.name: Mid-name labels with variable-width prefix: filenames2labels Extract pattern
evals.description: Derive class labels from a mid-name token in filenames where a leading prefix has variable width (some files start with subjNN, others with subjNNN). Targets filenames2labels with the Extract NV-pair using a pattern object (e.g. 'G' + digitsPattern). Probes the position-based slicing reflex AND the regexp/extractBetween cold reflex — a cold agent reaches for `regexp(name, '_(G\d+)_', 'tokens')` over `filenames2labels`, and the skill teaches the canonical one-call form. Eval went through three diagnosis stages on 2026-05-28: (1) prompt-strength rewrite (closed naming the landmark) → still +0 lift; (2) cold-agent probe outside harness confirmed baseline reflex is regexp/extractBetween, NOT filenames2labels; (3) audit found genL3_pattern.m docstring literally named 'filenames2labels with an Extract pattern object' — eval-runner agents were reading the generator. Fix: stripped docstring to data-shape only, added explicit no-read-generator hard rule to spawn prompts. Post-fix sweep: baseline 40% (regexp + manual fileparts loop), with-skill 100%, lift +60pp. See 03-evaluation-report.md § 20 for the full diagnosis chain.
evals.prompt: I have a flat folder of .mat files containing accelerometer signals from a hand-gesture experiment. To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genL3_pattern;`. The filenames encode subject ID, gesture identifier, and trial number — example: `subj01_G3_trial07.mat`. Build a signalDatastore over dataDir and derive a categorical label per file from the gesture identifier. Print the per-class file count.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genL3_pattern to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Uses filenames2labels to derive labels from filenames
assertions.expected: filenames2labels

assertions.description: Passes the Extract NV-pair to filenames2labels
assertions.expected: Extract

assertions.description: Does not hand-roll filename parsing with extractBetween / extractBefore / extractAfter / regexp on filenames
assertions.expected: Solution does not derive labels by string surgery on file paths (extractBetween/extractAfter/extractBefore on the filename, regexp returning capture groups, sscanf, or split('_') on each filename). filenames2labels(sds, Extract=pattern) is the canonical one-call form.

assertions.description: Does not slice by fixed character positions (which would fail on mixed-width subj prefix)
assertions.expected: Solution does not extract the gesture code by indexing fixed character positions in the filename (e.g., name(8:9), substring of length 2 starting at a hardcoded offset). The mixed-width subject prefix (subj01 vs subj004) defeats position-based slicing — the position of the gesture code shifts.

evals.name: Parallel processing across a parpool: partition(ds, N, k) dispatch
evals.description: Process a folder of signal files across N workers. Targets partition(ds, N, k) for per-worker datastore slicing. Probes the dominant L5b reflex of constructing a fresh signalDatastore(file) inside a parfor loop. ACCEPTED REGRESSION CHECK: this eval showed +0 lift across three diagnosis rounds on 2026-05-28 (prompt-strength rewrite, generator-source rule fix, prompt-structural audit). Cold-agent probe outside the harness reached for parfor first, parfeval/spmd next, partition(ds,N,k) only as option 4 with the caveat 'overkill for 60 files' — confirming the discrimination signal exists. But once the prompt frames the task as 'build a signalDatastore over dataDir, then for k = 1..4 shows what each worker would receive', partition(sds, N, k) is the only function whose signature shape matches that question. Further prompt stripping would degrade realism. Retained as a regression guard: if with-skill score ever drops below 100%, the canonical partition recipe regressed in the skill text. See 03-evaluation-report.md § 20 for the full diagnosis chain.
evals.prompt: I have a folder of 60 .mat signal files. To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genL5b_parallel;`. I want to split the work across 4 parallel workers — each worker should get its own non-overlapping subset of the files (~15 files each). Write a script that builds a signalDatastore over dataDir, then for k = 1..4 shows what each worker would receive: the per-worker file count and the size of one read from that worker's portion. Just iterate serially — don't spin up a parpool.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genL5b_parallel to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Uses partition to slice the datastore per worker
assertions.expected: partition

assertions.description: Calls partition with the (datastore, N, k) signature, not numpartitions alone
assertions.expected: Solution calls partition(sds, N, k) inside the per-worker loop to obtain a sub-datastore. numpartitions can be used to query the natural partition count, but the dispatch step itself must be partition(sds, N, k). A solution that only calls numpartitions without a follow-up partition(sds, N, k) does not satisfy this assertion.

assertions.description: Does not construct a fresh signalDatastore per file inside the worker loop
assertions.expected: Solution does not build a per-file signalDatastore inside the loop (e.g., signalDatastore(sds.Files{k}) or signalDatastore(file_for_worker_k)). The canonical pattern is to partition the existing datastore once with partition(sds, N, k) and let the sub-datastore drive the worker's reads.

assertions.description: Does not hand-roll worker file lists by manual index arithmetic on sds.Files
assertions.expected: Solution does not split work by computing each worker's file indices via floor/ceil arithmetic on numel(sds.Files) and then either reading via sds.Files{idx} or constructing per-worker datastores from those slices. partition(sds, N, k) handles the slicing intelligently; manual arithmetic discards that knowledge and is brittle.

evals.name: Knowledge probe: signalMask methods + signalDatastore CSV / NV-pair recall
evals.description: Direct factual knowledge check against the skill's API surface. No code execution, no MATLAB run — assertions are graded against the agent's textual answer. Targets four high-value coverage points: (1) signalMask method-name knowledge with explicit hallucination defense (catmask exists, getmask does NOT); (2) the two CSV-fluency NV-pairs (SignalVariableNames, SampleRateVariableName); (3) MemberNames for per-file metadata (G14); (4) ReadOutputOrientation='row' to avoid the manual transpose tax (G15). This is the only knowledge-shaped eval; it provides a cheap regression check for hallucination-defense text drift.
evals.prompt:
Quick knowledge check on signalDatastore and signalMask — DO NOT write code, DO NOT run MATLAB, just answer in plain text:

1. signalMask is a Signal Processing Toolbox object you build from an ROI table. What method on signalMask returns a categorical per-sample mask (one categorical value per signal sample)? Is there a method called `getmask`?
2. For signalDatastore reading CSV files: which name-value argument tells the constructor which CSV column is the signal? Which name-value argument tells it which column carries the per-file sample rate?
3. For signalDatastore reading multivariate .mat files where each .mat has multiple variables you want to expose as named members per read: which name-value argument carries the per-file member names?
4. signalDatastore yields each read as a column-oriented signal by default. Which name-value argument flips this to row-oriented so you don't have to transpose every read?

Answer concisely. Quote skill text where you can.

context.setup: Knowledge probe — no setup, no MATLAB execution. Grader treats this eval as text-only and skips the code_executes assertion type.

assertions.description: Names catmask as the signalMask method that returns a categorical per-sample mask
assertions.expected: catmask

assertions.description: Explicitly states that signalMask does NOT have a method called getmask
assertions.expected: Answer explicitly says getmask does not exist on signalMask (or equivalent: 'no such method', 'is not a method'). A silent omission of getmask is not enough — this is the hallucination-defense regression test. Inventing a getmask alternative (e.g., 'use getmask which is documented as ...') fails this assertion.

assertions.description: Names SignalVariableNames as the NV-pair selecting the CSV signal column
assertions.expected: SignalVariableNames

assertions.description: Names SampleRateVariableName as the NV-pair recovering per-file sample rate
assertions.expected: SampleRateVariableName

assertions.description: Names MemberNames as the NV-pair carrying per-file member names
assertions.expected: MemberNames

assertions.description: Names ReadOutputOrientation as the NV-pair controlling row vs column orientation
assertions.expected: ReadOutputOrientation

assertions.description: Does not invent non-existent method names (getCatMask, getMask, mask2cat, etc.) for signalMask
assertions.expected: Answer does not name fabricated methods on signalMask such as getCatMask, getMask, mask2cat, toCategorical, asCategoricalMask, etc. Only catmask (and binmask, which exists) are documented. Mentioning binmask in passing is fine; inventing a non-existent name is not.

evals.name: Knowledge probe: signalDatastore NV-pair surface (open enumeration)
evals.description: Open-enumeration knowledge probe for the signalDatastore NV-pair / property surface (13 names). Pruned 2026-05-28 from a 15-assertion liberal draft using n=2 baseline + n=2 with-skill: dropped 6 assertions both arms passed (the names training already covers — IncludeSubfolders, FileExtensions, SignalVariableNames, ReadFcn, MemberNames, AlternateFileSystemRoots) and kept the 7 discriminators (h4-h6 time-info trio, h9-h10 read controls, h12-h13 R2024b output controls) plus the two cheap regression guards. With-skill 8/8 (2/2 each), baseline 4.5/8 avg.
evals.prompt:
I'm about to wire up a `signalDatastore` for an ML training pipeline. Before I write code, I want to make sure I'm aware of every NV-pair the constructor exposes -- both the common ones and the ones that are easy to miss. Walk me through them with a one-line gloss for each, so I can decide which ones I actually need.

Just list the NV-pairs in plain text -- don't write or run code yet. If you're unsure whether a name is real, flag it rather than inventing.

context.setup: Knowledge probe — no MATLAB execution. Grader scores against the live-doc 13-item surface plus a fabricated-name list.

assertions.description: Names SampleRateVariableName
assertions.expected: SampleRateVariableName

assertions.description: Names SampleTimeVariableName
assertions.expected: SampleTimeVariableName

assertions.description: Names TimeValuesVariableName
assertions.expected: TimeValuesVariableName

assertions.description: Names ReadOutputOrientation
assertions.expected: ReadOutputOrientation

assertions.description: Names ReadSize
assertions.expected: ReadSize

assertions.description: Names OutputEnvironment
assertions.expected: OutputEnvironment

assertions.description: Names OutputDataType
assertions.expected: OutputDataType

assertions.description: Does not name SignalVariableNames as 'VariableNames' (skill explicitly flags this confusion)
assertions.expected: Answer uses 'SignalVariableNames' verbatim. The skill calls out 'SignalVariableNames (not VariableNames)' as a gotcha — wrong name silently falls through to default behavior.

assertions.description: Does not invent fabricated NV-pair names
assertions.expected: Answer does not list fabricated names such as SignalLengthDimension (the eval-06 hallucination), VariableNames, SignalColumn, SampleRateColumn, OrientReadOutput, SignalDimension, OutputOrientation, ReadOrientation, etc. If uncertain, agent says so rather than inventing. Open enumeration must not become open invention.

evals._note: Flavor-C v2 rewrite of eval-09 (labels-family). v1 leaked function names in the prompt ('I'm wiring up framelbl/splitlabels/countlabels'), letting the bare agent run `help <name>` to enumerate input types and NV-pairs. v2 describes user data and intended outcome only — no function names. Agent must produce the function name AND the discriminating NV-pair from the goal alone. With no function name in the prompt, `help <whatever>` returns 'function not found' (or sends agent on a fishing trip we can detect). Each item: agent must name (a) the function and (b) the NV-pair if relevant. Items 7-8 remain regression checks for the input-type widening (logical / numeric label sources are accepted by these helpers — the v0.2 probe found bare agents asserting 'categorical-only').
evals.name: Knowledge probe: labels-family API selection from user goals (v2, name-blind)
evals.description: Task-anchored knowledge probe for the labels-family helpers (countlabels / splitlabels / framelbl). For each user-goal scenario, the agent must name the right MATLAB function (and discriminating NV-pair if applicable) with a one-line gloss — without any function name appearing in the prompt. Replaces the v1 flavor-C draft, which leaked the function names ('I'm wiring up framelbl, splitlabels, and countlabels...') so a bare agent could enumerate the answer key via `help`. Goal→name mapping with no name in the prompt has no enumerable answer key; the agent has to know the canonical names from prior knowledge or admit it doesn't. Known harness limitation: under the current qualification harness, the bare arm has access to mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code and can run `help functionName` to enumerate the answer key. Reported bare scores on this eval are therefore introspection-aided recall, not pure model recall, and the lift number is a lower bound. To be re-measured when the harness supports per-eval allowedTools restriction.
evals.prompt:
I have a labelled signal dataset and I'm preparing it for ML training in MATLAB R2024b+. For each scenario below, name the MATLAB function I should call (and the NV-pair argument if one is involved), with a one-line gloss on what it does. Pick the right one — don't list a whole API surface. If a scenario is solved by combining helpers, name the primary function for that scenario.

1. I have a datastore of MAT files where each file's first variable is a categorical label vector. Before I split into train/val/test I want to know how many files belong to each class so I can check for class imbalance.
2. I have a combined-datastore whose first underlying datastore yields signals and whose second underlying datastore yields the per-sample categorical label for each signal. I want a stratified train / val / test split. Name the function and the argument that tells it which underlying datastore holds the labels.
3. I have a MATLAB table where one column is the per-row signal data and another column is the per-row categorical label. I want a stratified train / val / test split. Name the function and the argument that tells it which table column holds the labels.
4. For each long signal I have an ROI table (start time, end time, class) plus a sample rate. I want to derive a per-frame label vector aligned to a frame size and frame overlap, so I can train a frame-level classifier.
5. For each long signal I have a per-sample categorical label vector (one label per sample, same length as the signal). I want to derive a per-frame label vector aligned to a frame size and frame overlap, so I can train a frame-level classifier.
6. I have a categorical label vector and want a quick per-class count summary to inspect for class imbalance.
7. My label source is a logical vector (true / false per sample) and I want to feed it directly to the per-class count helper without converting it to categorical first. Is that supported, and which function handles it?
8. My label source is a numeric vector of class indices (1, 2, 3, ...) and I want to feed it directly to the stratified-split helper without converting it to categorical first. Is that supported, and which function handles it?

Answer as a numbered list. For each item: `function-name` (and `NV-pair` if relevant) — one-line gloss. If you can't recall the canonical name, write 'uncertain' rather than inventing.

context.setup: Knowledge probe — no MATLAB execution required. Each scenario has a single canonical answer; bare and with-skill agents are graded on whether they produce that name. Function names are deliberately absent from the prompt so the agent cannot use `help <name>` to enumerate the answer.

assertions.description: Item 1 names countlabels (datastore input)
assertions.expected: Item 1 names 'countlabels' as the answer (with mention or implication that it accepts datastore input). Not acceptable: 'tabulate', 'histcounts', 'classCounts', 'summary', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer (failure but not invention).

assertions.description: Item 2 names splitlabels with UnderlyingDatastoreIndex NV-pair
assertions.expected: Item 2 names 'splitlabels' as the function and 'UnderlyingDatastoreIndex' as the NV-pair pointing to the labels-bearing underlying datastore. Not acceptable: 'cvpartition', 'splitData', 'splitEachLabel', invented NV-pair names like 'LabelDatastoreIndex' or 'UnderlyingDatastore'. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 3 names splitlabels with TableVariable NV-pair
assertions.expected: Item 3 names 'splitlabels' as the function and 'TableVariable' as the NV-pair pointing to the labels column. Not acceptable: 'TableColumn' (the documented confused name), 'LabelColumn', 'LabelVariable', 'cvpartition', any invented NV-pair name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 4 names framelbl with ROI-table input
assertions.expected: Item 4 names 'framelbl' as the function. Bonus if it also mentions that 'SampleRate' is required as a positional/NV argument when the input is an ROI table. Not acceptable: 'roi2framelbl', 'sigroi2framelbl', 'frameROI', 'labelFrames', 'framesig', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 5 names framelbl with categorical-vector input
assertions.expected: Item 5 names 'framelbl' as the function (with categorical-vector / per-sample-label input). Not acceptable: 'framesig', 'seq2framelbl', 'segmentLabels', 'frameSamples', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 6 names countlabels (categorical-vector input)
assertions.expected: Item 6 names 'countlabels' as the function. Not acceptable: 'tabulate', 'histcounts', 'summary', 'classCounts', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 7 confirms logical input is accepted as a label-source by countlabels (not categorical-only)
assertions.expected: Item 7 names 'countlabels' AND confirms it accepts logical input as a label source (or at minimum does NOT assert 'must convert to categorical first' or 'categorical only'). Acceptable: 'countlabels supports logical input directly', 'countlabels accepts logical labels'. Not acceptable: 'must convert to categorical first', 'countlabels is categorical-only'. The skill explicitly widens the documented input-type list — this is the headline regression check. 'uncertain' is acceptable here only if the agent does not assert categorical-only.

assertions.description: Item 8 confirms numeric input is accepted by splitlabels (not categorical-only)
assertions.expected: Item 8 names 'splitlabels' AND confirms numeric class-index vectors are accepted (or at minimum does NOT assert 'must convert to categorical first' or 'categorical only'). Acceptable: 'splitlabels supports numeric class-index vectors', 'numeric class indices work directly'. Not acceptable: 'splitlabels is categorical-only', 'must convert to categorical first'. Companion regression check to j7. 'uncertain' is acceptable only if the agent does not assert categorical-only.

assertions.description: Aggregate coverage (>=6/8 items correctly named)
assertions.expected: At least 6 of items 1-8 produce the canonical function name (and the discriminating NV-pair where called for). 'uncertain' and invented names both fail. Headline coverage metric — measures whether the agent confidently produces canonical names rather than disclaiming or inventing.

assertions.description: Does not produce iconic invented names as canonical answers
assertions.expected: TableColumn|LabelColumn|InputLabelType|LabelSource|CategoricalArray|LabelDatastoreIndex|categorical only|categorical-only|must convert to categorical|categorical input only|seq2framelbl|roi2framelbl|sigroi2framelbl

evals._note: Flavor-C v2 rewrite of eval-10 (canonical name discrimination). v1 mentioned 'signalDatastore', 'signalMask', and 'framelbl' in the prompt, letting the bare agent run `methods('signalMask')` / `help framelbl` to enumerate the answer key. v2 describes user data and intended outcome only — no MATLAB function/method/object names anywhere. Agent must produce the canonical name from the goal alone. With no name in the prompt, `help <whatever>` returns 'function not found' and `methods()` requires the agent to know the parent object first — the very thing being tested.
evals.name: Knowledge probe: API selection from user goals (v2, name-blind)
evals.description: Task-anchored knowledge probe. For each user-goal scenario, the agent must name the canonical Signal Processing Toolbox function or method (and parent class/object if a method) from the goal alone — no function, method, or object names appear in the prompt. Replaces the v1 flavor-C draft which named signalMask/framelbl/signalDatastore explicitly and let the bare agent enumerate the answer key via `help`/`methods`/`which`. Goal→name mapping with no name in the prompt has no enumerable answer key. Known harness limitation: under the current qualification harness, the bare arm has access to mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code and can run `help functionName` to enumerate the answer key. Reported bare scores on this eval are therefore introspection-aided recall, not pure model recall, and the lift number is a lower bound. To be re-measured when the harness supports per-eval allowedTools restriction.
evals.prompt:
I'm building an ML training pipeline in MATLAB R2024b+ that ingests labelled signals, frames them, and prepares them for `trainnet`. For each task below, name the function or method I should reach for (and the parent class / object if it's a method on an object), with a one-line gloss on what it does. Pick the right one — don't list a whole API surface.

1. I have a per-sample binary mask (0/1 indicating event-present-or-not for each sample of a signal). I want to convert it into a per-sample categorical-class mask (every sample tagged with its class label) using a MATLAB-toolbox object designed for signal labels.
2. I already have one of those signal-label objects holding categorical regions. I want the binary (0/1) per-sample mask form back out of it.
3. I have a categorical label per sample (one label for every signal sample) and want to derive one label per analysis frame at a given frame size and frame overlap.
4. I have an ROI table (one row per region, with a start time, end time, and class column) and a sample rate. I want to derive one label per analysis frame at a given frame size and frame overlap, aligned with the timing of the ROIs.
5. In task 4, multiple ROIs may overlap inside the same frame. I want to break ties by a fixed class-priority order so each frame still gets exactly one label. Name the NV-pair argument that controls this.
6. I have a categorical label vector and want a stratified train / val / test split into fractions (e.g., 0.7 / 0.15 / 0.15) in one call.
7. I have a categorical label vector (or a datastore yielding labels) and want a per-class count summary as a table.
8. I have a folder of signal files where each file's parent folder name is its class label (one folder per class). I want a categorical label per file derived from the parent folder name automatically — not a hand-rolled `fileparts` loop.
9. I have a folder of signal files where the class label is encoded inside the filename itself (e.g., between two delimiters). I want a categorical label per file derived from a pattern-extraction on the filename automatically.

Answer as a numbered list. For each item: `name` (parent class/object if method) — one-line gloss. If you can't recall the canonical name, write 'uncertain' rather than inventing.

context.setup: Knowledge probe — no MATLAB execution required. Function, method, and object names are deliberately absent from the prompt so the agent cannot use `help <name>` or `methods(<class>)` to enumerate the answer.

assertions.description: Item 1 names catmask (signalMask method) and identifies signalMask as the parent
assertions.expected: Item 1 names 'catmask' as the method AND identifies 'signalMask' as the parent object. Acceptable phrasing: 'catmask (signalMask method)' or 'signalMask.catmask'. Not acceptable: 'getmask', 'mask2cat', 'tocategorical', 'binmask' (wrong direction), any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer (failure but not invention).

assertions.description: Item 2 names binmask (signalMask method)
assertions.expected: Item 2 names 'binmask' as the method on signalMask. Acceptable: 'binmask (signalMask method)' or 'signalMask.binmask'. Not acceptable: 'getmask', 'getBinaryMask', 'logicalMask', 'catmask' (wrong direction), any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 3 names framelbl (categorical-vector input mode)
assertions.expected: Item 3 names 'framelbl' as the function. Acceptable phrasing: 'framelbl' standalone or 'framelbl (with categorical-vector input)'. Not acceptable: 'framesig', 'frameSig' (wrong — frames signal data, not labels), 'seq2framelbl', 'segmentLabels', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 4 names framelbl (ROI-table input mode)
assertions.expected: Item 4 names 'framelbl' as the function (same function, ROI-table input mode). Bonus if it mentions the 'SampleRate' positional/NV argument required when input is an ROI table. Not acceptable: 'roi2framelbl', 'sigroi2framelbl', 'labelFrames', 'frameROI', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 5 names PriorityList NV-pair on framelbl
assertions.expected: Item 5 names 'PriorityList' as the NV-pair argument. Not acceptable: 'LabelPriorityOrder', 'PriorityOrder', 'ClassPriority', 'TieBreaker', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 6 names splitlabels
assertions.expected: Item 6 names 'splitlabels' as the function. Bonus if it mentions the 'randomized' positional flag for stratified randomized splits. Not acceptable: 'cvpartition', 'splitData', 'splitEachLabel', 'trainTestSplit', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 7 names countlabels
assertions.expected: Item 7 names 'countlabels' as the function. Not acceptable: 'tabulate', 'histcounts', 'classCounts', 'summary', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 8 names folders2labels
assertions.expected: Item 8 names 'folders2labels' as the function. Not acceptable: 'foldernames2labels', 'parentFolderLabels', 'foldersToLabels', 'dirLabels', hand-rolled fileparts loop, any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Item 9 names filenames2labels with extract NV-pair
assertions.expected: Item 9 names 'filenames2labels' as the function. Bonus if it mentions an NV-pair like 'ExtractBefore', 'ExtractAfter', or 'ExtractBetween' for the pattern-based extraction. Not acceptable: 'regexpFilenames', 'patternLabels', 'filenamesToLabels', any invented name. 'uncertain' is a non-answer.

assertions.description: Aggregate coverage (>=7/9 items correctly named)
assertions.expected: At least 7 of items 1-9 produce the canonical name (not 'uncertain', not an invented variant). Headline coverage metric — measures whether the agent confidently produces canonical names rather than disclaiming or inventing.

assertions.description: Does not produce iconic invented names as canonical answers
assertions.expected: getmask|LabelPriorityOrder|SampleRateColumn|OrientReadOutput|mask2cat|SignalLengthDimension|TableColumn|InputLabelType|seq2framelbl|roi2framelbl|sigroi2framelbl|foldernames2labels|filenamesToLabels

evals.name: WAV folder without Audio Toolbox: signalDatastore + custom ReadFcn fallback
evals.description: Build a signalDatastore over a folder of .wav files when Audio Toolbox is NOT available. Targets the custom-ReadFcn fallback documented in references/wf-custom-readfcn.md (signalDatastore + ReadFcn=@readWav calling base-MATLAB audioread). Sourced from QE feedback 2026-05-29: the QE's MATLAB had only Signal + Wavelet, the agent's first instinct was audioDatastore (Audio Toolbox), and recovery to the canonical fallback took several iterations. This eval makes the constraint explicit and asserts the agent reaches the correct fallback path on first try. Probes: (a) the audioDatastore reflex when Audio Toolbox is unavailable; (b) whether the custom-ReadFcn workflow file is reachable from SKILL.md's softened 'When NOT to Use' carve-out.
evals.prompt: I have a folder of .wav files. To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genWavNoAudioToolbox;`. I do not have and cannot use Audio Toolbox — only Signal Processing Toolbox is available. Build a signalDatastore over dataDir that reads the .wav data and recovers the per-file sample rate. Show how to read one file and obtain both the signal and its sample rate.

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genWavNoAudioToolbox to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end to end without error
Routes to the canonical fallback: signalDatastore + custom ReadFcn calling base-MATLAB audioread (no audioDatastore)
assertions.expected: Single-assertion routing check: the script constructs a signalDatastore, supplies a ReadFcn NV-pair, and the ReadFcn body calls audioread. audioDatastore (Audio Toolbox) must not appear. Routing is table stakes here — passing this on its own is not value-add; the discriminating assertions are the framework-contract ones below.

assertions.description: ReadFcn's data return is the raw signal (numeric array), not a wrapper struct or cell that bundles signal + Fs together
assertions.expected: The ReadFcn returns the audio samples as the first output directly — i.e. `data` is a numeric array. Wrapping the signal inside a struct (`out = struct('Signal',y,'SampleRate',fs)`) or a cell (`out = {y, fs}`) and returning that as the single output FAILS this assertion. The framework reserves the data slot for the signal itself and the info slot for metadata; bundling them defeats the contract and breaks downstream consumers (transform/writeall/tall) that expect data to be the signal.

assertions.description: ReadFcn declares 2 outputs [data, info] and populates info.SampleRate from audioread's fs
assertions.expected: The ReadFcn signature is `function [data, info] = readWav(filename)` (two outputs). It captures fs from `[data, fs] = audioread(filename)` and assigns it to `info.SampleRate`. A 1-output ReadFcn — even one that returns a struct or cell containing fs — FAILS this assertion. The 2-output signature is what surfaces metadata through the framework's info channel.

assertions.description: Caller reads the datastore with [data, info] = read(sds) and recovers the sample rate from info.SampleRate
assertions.expected: The script calls `read(sds)` (or `[data, info] = read(sds)`) with two output arguments and accesses `info.SampleRate` to recover Fs. Solutions that read with one output and unpack a wrapper (`item = read(sds); item.SampleRate` or `out = read(sds); out{2}`) FAIL this assertion — they bypass the framework's info channel. Re-calling `audioread(filename)` after the read to recover fs also FAILS.

assertions.description: Does not consume the datastore output by positional cell indexing (out{1}, out{2})
assertions.expected: The solution does not unpack the datastore read result via cell indexing such as `out{1}` for signal and `out{2}` for sample rate. Positional cell indexing is brittle (no field name, magic numbers) and indicates the agent invented its own data shape instead of using the framework's [data, info] contract. FAILS if the script contains expressions like `out{1}`, `out{2}`, `item{1}`, or `item{2}` reading from a signalDatastore read result.

assertions.description: Does not set info.FileName to a string scalar (silent-NaN landmine on info.SampleRate)
assertions.expected: If info.FileName is set in the ReadFcn, it must be a char vector — never a string scalar. info.FileName = string(filename) silently NaN's info.SampleRate downstream. Best practice: omit info.FileName entirely (the framework supplies it). FAILS only if the ReadFcn explicitly assigns a string scalar to info.FileName.

evals.name: In-file string labels: pin category set across train/val splits before trainnet
evals.description: Build a stratified split + transform pipeline over a folder of .mat files where the label is stored as a string scalar (not pre-converted to categorical). Wire the validation datastore into trainnet and run at least one iteration. Sourced from manager feedback 2026-05-29: a real workflow run hit `Categories of the validation data must match the categories of the training data.` because the transform did `categorical(string(c{2}))` per observation — each observation's categorical contained only the one category present, so train/val category sets diverged. The canonical fix is to extract `cats = categories(labels)` upfront from the full label vector and pin it inside the transform: `categorical(string(c{2}), cats)`. This eval probes whether the agent reaches that fix on first try, or whether trainnet errors at the validation-data category-check (which fires before the first training iteration). Targets the transform-to-trainnet boundary covered by wf-handoff-to-dl.md; a sibling gap to the 'predictor [T C] vs [C T] orientation' gotcha already in the file.
evals.prompt: I have a folder of .mat files. To materialize the data, run `addpath('evals/files'); dataDir = genStringLabelMat;`. Each file contains three variables: `data` (a 200x4 multi-channel signal), `lbl` (a string scalar holding the class), and `Fs` (the sample rate). Build a stratified 70/15/15 train/val/test split over these files and train a small 1-D CNN via `trainnet`, with the validation datastore wired in via `ValidationData=...`. Use `MaxEpochs=1` and `MiniBatchSize=4` so the run is short. Show that training starts (one iteration of the optimizer is enough — full convergence is not required).

context.setup: Add evals/files to the path, run dataDir = genStringLabelMat to generate the dataset, then operate on dataDir.

assertions.description: Generated script runs end-to-end without error — trainnet does not abort at the validation-data category-check or anywhere else
assertions.expected: Script reaches the trainnet call and trainnet completes at least one iteration of the optimizer without erroring. The bug under test fires as a runtime error before the first iteration: `Categories of the validation data must match the categories of the training data.` PASS = no such error (and no other runtime error). FAIL = any runtime error during the trainnet call.

assertions.description: Transform pins the full category set when converting string -> categorical (canonical fix for the train/val mismatch)
assertions.expected: Inside the transform applied to the train and val datastores, the string-to-categorical conversion is `categorical(<str>, cats)` where `cats` is a fixed list extracted from the full label vector before splitting (e.g. `cats = categories(labels)`). FAIL if the transform does `categorical(<str>)` or `categorical(string(<str>))` without pinning a category set — that produces per-observation categoricals with only the one present category, which diverge across train/val and trigger the runtime error in L1.

assertions.description: Stratified split via splitlabels + subset (regression check for eval-01 territory)
assertions.expected: Uses splitlabels to produce split indices over the labels and subset(sds, idx) to apply them to the datastore. FAIL if the script reaches for cvpartition or hand-rolled randperm-based splitting.

assertions.description: Does not reach for filenames2labels or folders2labels (labels are in-file, not encoded in filenames or folders)
assertions.expected: The label-derivation path uses signalDatastore + SignalVariableNames="lbl" (or equivalent in-file extraction). FAIL if filenames2labels or folders2labels appears in the solution — those would silently miscategorize when filenames carry no class signal.

assertions.description: trainingOptions includes ValidationData (the prompt-forced setting that makes the bug fire)
assertions.expected: ValidationData

assertions.description: Solution uses signalDatastore (gate; routing check)
assertions.expected: signalDatastore
